guardrails.rule: No writes to affected DM partitions until DM restore protocol completes and integrity check passes
DM data-loss recovery requires explicit approval from DM service owner and PFR DRI before execution
Verify no active transactions are in flight on affected DM nodes before initiating data restore; quiesce workload first

content.text: [PFR] DM Dataloss Drill – April 2026. This document covers the PilotFish Recovery (PFR) drill for a scoped Data Management (DM) data-loss scenario. The drill simulates a data-loss event on DM, validating detection, escalation, recovery procedures, and data integrity verification. Covers pre-drill readiness checks, fault injection steps, DM restore sequence, partition integrity validation, and post-recovery sign-off criteria. Comprehensive drill report spanning 15 pages (~2482 words). DRM key issued 2026-04-07 (UTC). Authored by Banreet Kaur. NOTE: Source document is MIP-protected (Confidential – Internal Only); all structured fields are derived from document filename, OLE metadata, and MIP label data as the ciphertext is inaccessible without tenant credentials.
